info.name: Fleet Manager – Supabase API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Fleet Manager Next.js + Supabase project.

---

> ⚠️ **AI-GENERATED DRAFT — NOT YET REVIEWED**
> This collection was generated from automated codebase analysis (April 2026) using GitHub Copilot.
> It has not been fully verified by the development team. Treat all descriptions, validations, and flow notes as a starting point only.
>
> **Review template:** When you verify an endpoint, add a note in the request description using this format:
> `✅ Reviewed by [Name] on [Date] — [Confirmed correct / Notes on discrepancy]`

---

### Architecture Overview
This project uses three layers for its API:

| Layer | Technology | Equivalent in Spring Boot |
|---|---|---|
| **Data visibility** | Supabase RLS Policies | `@PreAuthorize` / Spring Security |
| **Business logic** | Supabase Edge Functions (Deno/TypeScript) | `@Service` layer |
| **Read-only DB logic** | Postgres RPCs (PL/pgSQL) | Repository query methods |
| **Next.js API routes** | `/app/api/**` | `@RestController` |

**Key rule:** RLS policies control *who can see which rows*. Business rules (time gates, ownership checks, duplicate prevention) live in Edge Functions.

**Service role key:** Using `service_role_key` as `apikey` bypasses ALL RLS policies — equivalent to running as root. Never use it client-side.

### Authentication Flow
Parents (Flutter app) and admins (web dashboard) both authenticate via Supabase Auth JWT. However:
- **Admins** have a role in `user_roles` → `rbac_notifications_select` policy grants access to all notifications
- **Parents** have NO role — their access is controlled solely by `notifications_select_own` policy (`recipient_user_id = auth.uid()`)

### Quick-start
1. Import both this file and `FleetManager.postman_environment.json`.
2. Select the **Fleet Manager – Supabase** environment.
3. Fill in `base_url`, `anon_key`, and `service_role_key`.
4. Run **Auth › Sign In** first — the test script auto-saves `auth_token` and `user_id`.
5. All authenticated requests will pick up `{{auth_token}}` automatically.

### Key variables
| Variable | Usage |
|---|---|
| `{{base_url}}` | `https://YOUR_PROJECT_REF.supabase.co` |
| `{{anon_key}}` | Passed as `apikey` header on all requests (RLS enforced) |
| `{{service_role_key}}` | Bypasses RLS — admin/service use only, never client-side |
| `{{auth_token}}` | Bearer JWT, auto-set after Sign In |
| `{{user_id}}` | Auth UUID, auto-set after Sign In |

item.name: Auth
item.description: Supabase Auth endpoints. Run **Sign In** first to populate {{auth_token}} and {{user_id}}.

request.description:
Exchange email + password for a JWT access_token.

The test script auto-saves `auth_token` and `user_id` to the environment.
Returns the authenticated user object for the current JWT. Corresponds to `supabase.auth.getUser()` / `auth.getSession()` used on every API route to validate the session.
Invalidates the current session JWT. Corresponds to `supabase.auth.signOut()` called in Topbar.tsx and Sidebar.tsx.
Sends a password-reset email. Corresponds to `supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail(email, { redirectTo })` called in `app/dashboard/admin/users/page.tsx`.

item.name: Notifications
item.description:
Manage system notifications. Most endpoints require `{{auth_token}}`.

Key table: `notifications`.

### Notification types
| Type | Inserted by | Who sees it |
|---|---|---|
| `certificate_expiry` | `create_certificate_notifications` RPC | Admins (RBAC) |
| `vehicle_breakdown` | `report_vehicle_breakdown` RPC | Admins (RBAC) |
| `driver_tardiness` | `/api/tardiness` Next.js route | Admins (RBAC) |
| `trip_cancellation` | `cancel-trip` Edge Function | Parent who cancelled + Admins |
| `trip_restored` | `restore-trip` Edge Function (planned) | Parent + Admins |

### RLS policies on `notifications`
| Policy | Role | Rule | Plain English |
|---|---|---|---|
| `notifications_select_own` | authenticated | `recipient_user_id = auth.uid()` | User sees only rows addressed to them (parents) |
| `rbac_notifications_select` | authenticated | `auth_has_any_permission([...])` | Dashboard users with `compliance/routes/passengers` permissions see ALL rows |
| `Allow anon read notifications by token` | anon | `email_token IS NOT NULL` | Unauthenticated email-link requests see token-bearing rows |

> ⚠️ AI-GENERATED DRAFT — NOT YET REVIEWED

request.description: Fetch a single notification record by ID. Used in resolve, dismiss, send-email, and get-recipients routes.
Returns count of notifications that need admin attention. Used by `useNotificationCount` hook for the badge counter in the sidebar.
Count of certificate-expiry compliance notifications. Used by `useComplianceNotificationCount` hook.
Count of breakdown/tardiness notifications. Used by `useRouteActivityNotificationCount` hook.
Marks a notification as resolved. Called by `POST /api/notifications/resolve`. Also updates the linked `vehicle_breakdowns` record if `notification_type = vehicle_breakdown`.
Dismisses a notification without resolving it. Called by `POST /api/notifications/dismiss`.
Updates notification after email is dispatched (`POST /api/notifications/send-email`).
Records the employee's response (booked appointment or uploaded document). Sets `admin_response_required = true` to trigger admin notification badge.
Next.js API route that calls the `create_certificate_notifications` Postgres RPC. Regenerates all certificate-expiry notifications based on current document expiry dates.
Checks whether the `create_certificate_notifications` PostgreSQL function exists. Uses `rpc/pg_get_function_identity_arguments`.
Returns the list of email recipients for a notification. Joins `notifications → routes → employees` to find driver and PA emails.
Builds and returns the HTML email template for a notification, including vehicle registration and employee name.
Sends an email for a notification and updates its status to `sent`. Also places a hold on the related entity (vehicle/driver/PA) if required.
Records a document_upload employee response on a notification and inserts a `system_activities` record.

Returns notifications visible to the authenticated user. No filter parameter needed — Postgres applies the correct RLS policy automatically based on the JWT.

**For parents (Flutter app):** Returns only `trip_cancellation` and `trip_restored` rows where `recipient_user_id = auth.uid()`. Enforced by the `notifications_select_own` RLS policy. Using service_role_key as `apikey` bypasses this — always use anon key client-side.

**For admins (dashboard):** Returns all rows. Enforced by `rbac_notifications_select` — requires any of: `compliance.read`, `compliance.write`, `reports.read`, `routes.read`, `routes.write`, `passengers.read`, `passengers.write`.

**Equivalent Spring Boot concept:** This is like a `@PreFilter` on a repository that rewrites the WHERE clause based on the user's role — invisible to the API caller.

**Expected response body for a parent:**
```json
[
  {
    "id": 150,
    "notification_type": "trip_cancellation",
    "status": "pending",
    "recipient_user_id": "<parent-uuid>",
    "details": {
      "passenger_name": "Mohammed Nawab",
      "route_number": "Oakwood AM Route 3",
      "session_type": "AM",
      "trip_date": "2026-04-02",
      "parent_name": "Margaret Thompson",
      "reason": "Child is unwell"
    },
    "created_at": "2026-04-02T00:14:43.193082"
  }
]
```

> ⚠️ AI-GENERATED DRAFT — NOT YET REVIEWED

item.name: Appointments
item.description:
Appointment slots and public booking flow via email token.

Tables: `appointment_slots`, `appointment_bookings`, `notifications`.

The booking endpoint (`/book`) is **public** (no auth) — it is accessed via a link in the notification email containing a one-time `token`.

request.description: Returns all appointment slots with their bookings. Called by `GET /api/appointments/slots`.
Creates a new bookable appointment slot. `created_by` is the `users.id` of the admin. Called by `POST /api/appointments/slots`.

item.name: Get Slot by ID (for booking validation)

request.description: Fetches slot timing before confirming a booking. Used within the public book endpoint. No auth required (public flow).
Checks if a slot is already booked before allowing a new booking. Returns 406/null if already taken.

Creates a booking for a slot. Public endpoint — no Authorization header, uses anon_key only. The token in the email is validated by first looking up `notifications.email_token`. Called by `POST /api/appointments/book`.

item.name: Validate Email Token (look up notification by token)

request.description: Validates the one-time token from the booking email link. Returns the notification record including entity info. Replace `TOKEN_FROM_EMAIL` with the actual token.

item.name: Vehicles
item.description:
Vehicle CRUD, hold/unhold, compliance calendar, seating plans.

Main table: `vehicles`. Related: `vehicle_breakdowns`, `vehicle_seating_plans`, `seating_plan_seats`, `vehicle_compliance_spare_assignments`.

Admin (service_role_key) requests are flagged individually.

request.description: Returns all vehicles ordered by creation date. From `lib/supabase/vehicles.ts → getVehicles()`.
Returns only vehicles flagged as spares.
Case-insensitive partial match on registration plate.
Minimal vehicle fetch used in notification email templates and appointment booking.
Places a vehicle on hold. Called by `POST /api/entities/hold` when `entity_type = vehicle`. Also cascades to hold the vehicle's assigned routes.
Clears a vehicle hold. Called by `POST /api/entities/unhold`.
Fetches all vehicle expiry date fields for the compliance calendar summary and day views.
Next.js API — aggregates vehicle compliance events for a date range. Joins vehicles, subject_documents, document_vehicle_links, document_expiry_tolerance.
Returns detailed compliance events for a single day, including vehicle details, assigned drivers, and document links.
Records assignment of a spare vehicle during a compliance period. Called by `POST /api/compliance/vehicles/calendar/assign-spare`.
Marks a spare assignment as reverted. Called by `POST /api/compliance/vehicles/calendar/revert-spare`.
Returns the currently active seating plan for a vehicle. From `lib/supabase/vehicleSeating.ts → getVehicleSeatingPlan()`.
All historical seating plans for a vehicle, newest first.
Deactivates a seating plan before creating/cloning a new one.

item.name: Clone Seating Plan to Another Vehicle (Next.js API)

request.description: Clones a seating plan (including all seats) from one vehicle to another. Deactivates any existing active plan on the target vehicle first.

item.name: Get Vehicle Field Audit Log (Next.js API)

request.description: Returns `field_audit_log` entries for a vehicle, with user email and employee full name. Ordered by `change_time` descending.
Routes assigned to a specific vehicle. Used in the TAS5 export flow and vehicle detail page.

item.name: Employees & Drivers
item.description:
Employee records, driver and passenger-assistant profiles, hold/unhold, compliance calendar, coordinator assignments.

Main tables: `employees`, `drivers`, `passenger_assistants`, `coordinator_school_assignments`, `field_audit_log`.

item.name: Get Employee Detail (with driver + PA joins)

request.description: Full employee record including all driver certification fields and PA badge info. Called by `GET /api/employees/[id]/detail`.
Look up an employee by personal email. Used in tardiness approval/decline to identify the coordinator.

item.name: Get Employee Field Audit Log (Next.js API)

request.description: Returns `field_audit_log` records for `table_name = employees`, with `users.email` and `employees.full_name`. Ordered by `change_time` desc.
Schools assigned to a coordinator employee. From `GET /api/employees/[id]/coordinator-schools`.
All driver expiry dates for the employee compliance calendar summary and day views.

item.name: Get Driver by Employee ID (incident export)

request.description: Driver badge number and full name for TR5/TR7 incident export forms.
Minimal driver list for the ManageSparesModal dropdown.
Places a driver on hold. Called by `POST /api/entities/hold` when `entity_type = driver`. Cascades to hold the driver's assigned routes.
Clears a driver hold. Called by `POST /api/entities/unhold`.
All PA expiry dates for compliance calendar summary and day views.
Returns the QR token and full name for a PA. Used in PassengerAssistantQRCode to generate a scannable QR code for document uploads.

item.name: Get PA by QR Token (public document upload)

request.description: Looks up a PA by QR token. Public endpoint (anon key only, no auth). Used in `lib/supabase/assistantDocuments.ts`.
Minimal PA list for the ManageSparesModal dropdown.
Places a PA on hold. Called by `POST /api/entities/hold` when `entity_type = passenger_assistant`.
Clears a PA hold. Called by `POST /api/entities/unhold`.
Next.js API — aggregates driver and PA certificate expiry events for a date range.
Detailed employee compliance events for a single day.

item.name: Routes
item.description:
Route records, hold/unhold cascade, TR1 export, field audit, spares.

Main table: `routes`. Related: `schools`, `drivers`, `passenger_assistants`, `vehicles`, `route_spares`.

request.description: Routes for a specific vehicle. Used in vehicle detail page and TAS5 export.
Gets driver and PA names/emails for routes assigned to a vehicle. Used in `GET /api/notifications/get-recipients`.
Places all routes for a vehicle on hold. Cascaded from `POST /api/entities/hold`. Can also filter by `driver_id` or `passenger_assistant_id`.
Clears hold on all routes for a vehicle. Cascaded from `POST /api/entities/unhold`.
Generates the TR1 SEN Transport Record. Fetches route + school + driver + PA + vehicle + passengers and fills the HTML template.

item.name: Get Route Field Audit Log (Next.js API)

request.description: Returns `field_audit_log` for `table_name = routes`, ordered by `change_time` desc.
Assigns a spare driver, PA, or vehicle to a route. `spare_type`: `driver` | `pa` | `vehicle`. From ManageSparesModal.
Calls `get_route_spares(p_route_id)` PostgreSQL function. Returns active spare assignments for a route. Used in `hooks/useRouteSpares.ts`.

item.name: Incidents
item.description:
Incident records and official form exports (TR5, TR6, TR7).

Main tables: `incidents`, `incident_employees`, `incident_passengers`, `route_sessions`, `documents`.

**All endpoints require `{{auth_token}}`.**

Export endpoints call the Next.js API which fetches all related data and fills the official HTML form template.

request.description: Full incident record with vehicle, route, and session details. Used in all three export routes.
All employees involved in an incident. Used in TR5 and TR7 exports.

item.name: Get Incident Passengers (TR5 – with passenger join)

request.description: Passengers involved in an incident. Used in TR5 export.

item.name: Get Incident Passengers (TR7 – with school join)

request.description: Passengers with school names for TR7 export.
Fetches the most recently saved TR5 form document URL for an incident. Same pattern applies for `TR6 Form` and `TR7 Form` — change `doc_type` accordingly.

Generates the TR5 Vehicle Incident Report. Fetches incident + vehicle + route + session + driver + PA + incident_employees + incident_passengers. Fills and returns the official HTML form. Saves a `documents` record with `doc_type = TR5 Form`.
Generates the TR6 form. Fetches incident + vehicle + route + session details. Saves a `documents` record with `doc_type = TR6 Form`.

Generates the TR7 SEN Pupil Incident Report. Fetches incident + vehicle + route + session + driver + PA + incident_employees + incident_passengers (with school names). Saves a `documents` record with `doc_type = TR7 Form`.

item.name: Tardiness & Breakdowns
item.description:
Driver tardiness reports (submit, approve, decline) and vehicle breakdown reports (report, find replacement, assign replacement).

Main tables: `tardiness_reports`, `vehicle_breakdowns`, `notifications`, `route_sessions`.

Breakdown RPCs: `report_vehicle_breakdown`, `find_replacement_vehicle`, `assign_replacement_vehicle`.

request.description: Inserts a `tardiness_reports` record and calls `create_tardiness_notification` RPC to generate a notification. Also checks for an existing `route_sessions` record for that date.
Direct insert into `tardiness_reports`. Use the Next.js API route instead in production — it also triggers the notification RPC.
Sets `tardiness_reports.status = approved`, records coordinator details, and resolves the linked `notifications` record.
Sets `tardiness_reports.status = declined`, records coordinator details, and resolves the linked notification.
Direct PostgREST PATCH. The filter `status=eq.pending` prevents double-processing. Use the Next.js API route in production.

Calls `report_vehicle_breakdown(p_route_session_id, p_description, p_location)`. Creates a `vehicle_breakdowns` record and a linked `notifications` record in one transaction. From `POST /api/breakdowns/report`.
Calls `find_replacement_vehicle(p_vehicle_id)`. Returns a list of available spare vehicles that can replace the broken-down vehicle. From `GET /api/breakdowns/[id]/replacement`.
Calls `assign_replacement_vehicle(p_breakdown_id, p_replacement_vehicle_id)`. Links the replacement vehicle to the breakdown record. From `POST /api/breakdowns/[id]/replacement`.
Marks a breakdown as resolved. Called as part of `POST /api/notifications/resolve` when `notification_type = vehicle_breakdown`.

item.name: Documents & Requirements
item.description:
Document requirements configuration, subject documents (certificates), document–entity link tables, document download, and the assistant QR document upload flow.

Main tables: `document_requirements`, `subject_documents`, `documents`, `document_vehicle_links`, `document_driver_links`, `document_pa_links`, `document_subject_document_links`.

Storage bucket download is handled by the Next.js API proxy at `/api/documents/view`.

request.description: All document requirement types. Optional filters: `subject_type`, `criticality`, `is_active`, `is_required`. Used in DynamicCertificatesForm and subject-documents API.

item.name: List Document Requirements – filtered by subject_type

request.description: Requirements filtered by subject type. Valid values: `driver`, `pa`, `vehicle`, `employee`.
Single requirement by ID. From `GET /api/admin/document-requirements/[id]`.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "TAS Badge",
  "code": "TAS",
  "subject_type": "driver",
  "requires_expiry": true,
  "requires_upload": true,
  "requires_number": true,
  "criticality": "high",
  "default_validity_days": 365,
  "renewal_notice_days": 30,
  "is_required": true,
  "is_active": true,
  "icon_path": null,
  "color": "#ef4444",
  "created_by": 42,
  "updated_by": 42
}

request.description: Creates a new document requirement type. From `POST /api/admin/document-requirements`.
Updates a document requirement. From `PATCH /api/admin/document-requirements/[id]`.
Deletes a requirement. The Next.js API first checks `subject_documents.count` for that `requirement_id` — if any exist it returns 409. From `DELETE /api/admin/document-requirements/[id]`.
Subject documents for a driver. Change filter to `pa_employee_id`, `vehicle_id`, or `employee_id` for other subject types. From `GET /api/admin/subject-documents`.
Creates a subject document (certificate record). From `POST /api/admin/subject-documents`.
Updates a subject document. From `PATCH /api/admin/subject-documents/[id]`.
Deletes a subject document record. From `DELETE /api/admin/subject-documents/[id]`.
Sets `status = valid` after a document file is uploaded via SubjectDocumentsChecklist. Browser client (anon key).
Inserts a document metadata record after a successful storage upload. From SubjectDocumentsChecklist and BadgePhotoUpload components. Returns `id` for subsequent link inserts.
Associates a document with a vehicle. Created after inserting the `documents` record. From SubjectDocumentsChecklist.
Associates a document with a driver. From SubjectDocumentsChecklist.
Associates a document with a passenger assistant. From SubjectDocumentsChecklist and assistants create/edit pages.
Links an uploaded file to its `subject_documents` certificate record. From SubjectDocumentsChecklist and assistants pages.

Streams a document from Supabase Storage through the Next.js server. Allowed buckets: `VEHICLE_DOCUMENTS`, `DRIVER_DOCUMENTS`, `PA_DOCUMENTS`, `EMPLOYEE_DOCUMENTS`, `MAINTENANCE_ICONS`. Requires `{{auth_token}}`.

Public RPC — no auth required. Called after a PA scans their QR badge and uploads a file. Validates the QR token, inserts a `documents` record, and creates the appropriate PA link. From `lib/supabase/assistantDocuments.ts`.

item.name: Calendar Notes
item.description:
Day-level notes on the compliance/operations calendar and view-tracking per user.

Main tables: `calendar_day_notes`, `calendar_day_note_views`.

All endpoints require `{{auth_token}}`.

request.description: Returns all calendar notes within a date range. From `GET /api/calendar/notes`.
Creates a new calendar note for a specific day. From `POST /api/calendar/notes`.
Updates the text of an existing calendar note. From `PATCH /api/calendar/notes`.
Deletes a specific note by ID. From `DELETE /api/calendar/notes`.

item.name: Delete All Notes for a Day

request.description: Deletes all notes for a given date. From `DELETE /api/calendar/notes` when `noteDate` param is provided.

item.name: Get Note View Status for User (date range)

request.description: Returns which dates in a range have been viewed by the current user. Used to show unread indicators on calendar days. From `GET /api/calendar/notes`.
Upserts a view record for a user and date. Conflict key: `(user_id, note_date)`. From `POST /api/calendar/notes/seen`.

item.name: Compliance Cases
item.description:
Compliance cases opened from expiry notifications, and the document-expiry tolerance configuration table.

Main tables: `compliance_cases`, `document_expiry_tolerance`.

All endpoints require `{{auth_token}}`.

item.name: Get Compliance Case by Notification ID

request.description: Fetch any existing compliance case tied to a notification. Used in the Compliance Case page to detect duplicate cases before creating a new one. From `GET /api/compliance`.
Opens a new compliance case. Called from the compliance case creation page. From `POST /api/compliance`.
Updates case status. Possible statuses: `open`, `in_progress`, `resolved`, `closed`. From `PATCH /api/compliance/[id]`.
Lists all open compliance cases with related notification info. From the compliance cases list page.
Retrieves all expiry-tolerance configuration rows used by the compliance calendar to determine warning windows per subject type. From `complianceCalendar.ts`.
Gets the tolerance window in days for a specific subject type + requirement combination.

item.name: Samsara / Telematics
item.description:
Vehicle telematics data ingested from Samsara, unmatched-vehicle management, and the admin sync/mapping tools.

Main tables: `vehicles_realtime`, `vehicle_locations`, `vehicle_telematics_latest`, `vehicle_telematics_history`, `route_points`, `samsara_vehicle_unmatched`, `samsara_sync_logs`, `samsara_mapping_audit_log`.

Note: `vehicles_realtime` and `vehicle_locations` selects use the **service_role_key** (bypasses RLS). All others use `{{auth_token}}`.

request.description: Real-time vehicle positions. SERVICE ROLE key required — this table is restricted in RLS. Used by LiveOperationsPanel and the map component.

item.name: Get Realtime Location for One Vehicle

request.description: Current position of a single vehicle. SERVICE ROLE key required.
Historical location feed. SERVICE ROLE key required.
Latest telematics snapshot for a vehicle keyed by Samsara ID. From Samsara webhook ingestion flow.
Upserts the realtime snapshot. Conflict key: `samsara_vehicle_id`. From Samsara webhook handler.
Appends a historical telematics row. Written by the Samsara webhook alongside the latest upsert.
Ordered stop sequence for an active route session. Used by the live map component.
Samsara vehicles whose `samsara_vehicle_id` has no match in the fleet. Shown in the admin Samsara mapping UI. From `GET /api/admin/samsara/mapping/unmatched`.
Upserts an unmatched vehicle record. Conflict key: `samsara_vehicle_id`.

Matches an unmatched Samsara vehicle to a fleet vehicle. Updates `vehicles.samsara_vehicle_id`, marks the row resolved in `samsara_vehicle_unmatched`, and inserts an audit entry in `samsara_mapping_audit_log`. From `POST /api/admin/samsara/mapping/resolve`.
Starts a sync log entry. After the sync completes, PATCH the row with `status`, `finished_at`, `vehicles_processed`, and any `errors`.

item.name: Dev Seed Samsara Data (Next.js internal API)

request.description: Development-only endpoint that seeds fake vehicle telemetry data into `vehicle_telematics_latest` and `vehicles_realtime`. From `POST /api/internal/samsara/dev-seed`.

item.name: Storage
item.description:
Supabase Storage bucket operations for fleet documents. Buckets: `ROUTE_DOCUMENTS`, `DRIVER_DOCUMENTS`, `PA_DOCUMENTS`, `VEHICLE_DOCUMENTS`, `EMPLOYEE_DOCUMENTS`, `MAINTENANCE_ICONS`.

File uploads are multipart `POST` to the Storage Upload API. Downloads are proxied through the Next.js `/api/documents/view` endpoint (see Documents folder).

All storage calls require the `{{auth_token}}` JWT.

request.description: Uploads a certificate PDF for a driver. Path pattern: `subject-documents/driver/{driver_employee_id}/{requirement_id}_{timestamp}_{index}_{randomhex}.{ext}`. Returns `Key` (storage path). Browser client (anon key).
Uploads a certificate file for a passenger assistant. Same path pattern as driver bucket.
Uploads a certificate file for a vehicle (e.g. MOT, insurance).
Uploads a certificate file for an office employee.

item.name: Upload to ROUTE_DOCUMENTS bucket (QR assistant upload)

request.description: Public upload from the PA QR scan page — no auth token, only `apikey`. Path pattern: `assistants/{pa_employee_id}/{docTypeSlug}_{timestamp}_{index}_{randomhex}.{ext}`. From `lib/supabase/assistantDocuments.ts`.
Uploads a custom maintenance type icon. Anon key only (public bucket).

item.name: Get Public URL for DRIVER_DOCUMENTS file

request.description: Retrieves the public URL for a driver document. This matches what `getPublicUrl(path)` returns from `storage.from('DRIVER_DOCUMENTS')`. For non-public buckets use the Next.js `/api/documents/view` proxy instead.
Removes a list of files from the bucket. Called during cleanup operations (e.g., when a subject document is deleted).

item.name: RPC Functions
item.description:
PostgreSQL RPC calls (`/rest/v1/rpc/...`) not already covered in other folders.

Some RPCs use the **service_role_key** as noted; all others use `{{auth_token}}`.

request.description: Saves the seating plan layout for a vehicle. From `InteractiveSeatingChart.tsx` via `PUT /api/vehicles/[id]/seating-plan`.
Returns available spare vehicles matching capacity and route date constraints. From `ReplacementVehicleFinder.tsx` and breakdown replacement flow.
Returns the current seat assignments for a route session including passenger details and seat positions. From `InteractiveSeatingChart.tsx`.
Assigns a passenger to a specific seat in a route session. Enforces uniqueness: clears any prior assignment for the same session+passenger. From `InteractiveSeatingChart.tsx`.
Removes a passenger's seat assignment from a route session. From `InteractiveSeatingChart.tsx`.
Scans all subject documents, identifies those expiring within the tolerance window, and creates notification rows. Called from the compliance calendar scheduler and the notification check API.
Exports TAS5 (driver timesheet) rows for an Excel export. SERVICE ROLE key required — RLS restricts this function. From `POST /api/routes/export-tas5`.
Creates a tardiness notification linked to a tardiness record. From `POST /api/tardiness/report`.
Full-text search over employees, drivers and PAs to find notification recipients. From `GET /api/notifications/get-recipients`.
Returns the permission set for the currently authenticated user. Used by `usePermissions` hook and `lib/permissions.ts` to gate UI features.

item.name: Edge Functions
item.description:
Supabase Edge Functions deployed under `/functions/v1/`.

All calls require `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` and the project-scoped `apikey` header.

Edge Functions run as Deno TypeScript on Supabase's infrastructure. They use the **service role key internally** to bypass RLS for writes, but they verify the caller's JWT themselves (step 1 of each function). This means business logic (ownership checks, time gates, duplicate prevention) is enforced in code — not in database policies.

> ⚠️ AI-GENERATED DRAFT — NOT YET REVIEWED

request.description:
Allows a registered parent to cancel their child's upcoming trip.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT of the parent (from Supabase Auth). The function verifies the token internally using the service role key.

### Request body
| Field | Type | Required | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `passenger_id` | number | ✅ | Must be a number |
| `session_type` | string | ✅ | Must be `"AM"` or `"PM"` |
| `trip_date` | string | ✅ | Format: `YYYY-MM-DD` |
| `reason` | string | ❌ | Optional free text |

### Business logic (in code — NOT in RLS)
1. JWT verified → parent identity established
2. `parent_contacts` table queried by `user_id = auth.uid()` — returns 401 if not a registered parent
3. `passenger_parent_contacts` junction checked — returns 403 if passenger not linked to this parent ("Not your child")
4. `passengers` + `routes` fetched to get departure time
5. **Time gate:** if `Date.now() >= route departure time`, returns 422 "Cancellation window has passed"
6. `route_sessions` checked — if session exists and `started_at` is set, returns 422 "Trip already in progress"
7. **Duplicate check:** if `parent_absence_reports` already has a row for this passenger+session, returns 409 "Already cancelled"
8. INSERT into `parent_absence_reports` (status: `new`)
9. INSERT into `notifications` (`notification_type: trip_cancellation`, `recipient_user_id: parent's auth UUID`)

### Expected responses
| Status | Body | Cause |
|---|---|---|
| 201 | `{ "absence_report_id": 123 }` | Success |
| 400 | `{ "error": "Missing or invalid fields..." }` | Bad request body |
| 401 | `{ "error": "Not authenticated" }` | Invalid/missing JWT |
| 401 | `{ "error": "Not a registered parent" }` | JWT valid but user not in `parent_contacts` |
| 403 | `{ "error": "Not your child" }` | passenger_id not linked to this parent |
| 404 | `{ "error": "Passenger not found" }` | passenger_id doesn't exist |
| 409 | `{ "error": "Already cancelled for this trip" }` | Duplicate cancellation |
| 422 | `{ "error": "Cancellation window has passed. Trip starts at HH:MM" }` | Too late to cancel |
| 422 | `{ "error": "Trip already in progress" }` | Session started |
| 422 | `{ "error": "Passenger has no assigned route" }` | Route not set up |

### Notification visibility after this call
The inserted `trip_cancellation` notification is visible to:
- **The parent** via `notifications_select_own` RLS policy (`recipient_user_id = auth.uid()`)
- **Admins** via `rbac_notifications_select` RLS policy (requires `passengers.read` or `routes.read` permission)

> ✅ Reviewed by Mahmood — April 2026 — Confirmed correct, deployed and tested end-to-end

Allows a parent to undo a previously submitted trip cancellation, restoring the child's place on the route.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT of the parent. The function verifies the token internally.

### Request body
| Field | Type | Required | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `passenger_id` | number | ✅ | Must be a number |
| `session_type` | string | ✅ | `"AM"` or `"PM"` |
| `trip_date` | string | ✅ | `YYYY-MM-DD` |

### Business logic (11 steps, in code — NOT in RLS)
1. **JWT verified** → parent identity established
2. **Body validated** → 400 if any field missing or wrong type
3. **Parent lookup** — `parent_contacts` queried by `user_id = auth.uid()` → 401 if not a registered parent
4. **Ownership check** — `passenger_parent_contacts` junction checked → 403 "Not your child"
5. **Passenger + route fetched** — to get departure time for the time gate
6. **Time gate** — if `Date.now() >= route departure time` → 422 "Restore window has passed. Trip starts at HH:MM"
7. **Route session lookup** — checks if the session exists and has `started_at` set → 422 "Trip already in progress"
8. **Find active absence report** — searches `parent_absence_reports` WHERE `passenger_id` matches AND `route_session_id` matches (or IS NULL if no session exists yet) AND `status IN ('new', 'acknowledged')`, ordered by `created_at DESC` → 404 "No active cancellation found for this trip" if nothing matches
9. **UPDATE absence report** — sets `status = 'cancelled'` (marks the absence as voided; the child is back on the route)
10. **INSERT notification** — `notification_type: trip_restored`, `recipient_user_id: parent's auth UUID`
11. **Return 200** with `{ absence_report_id }`

### Expected responses
| Status | Body | Cause |
|---|---|---|
| 200 | `{ "absence_report_id": 123 }` | Success — trip restored |
| 400 | `{ "error": "Missing or invalid fields..." }` | Bad request body |
| 401 | `{ "error": "Not authenticated" }` | Invalid/missing JWT |
| 401 | `{ "error": "Not a registered parent" }` | JWT valid but not in `parent_contacts` |
| 403 | `{ "error": "Not your child" }` | passenger_id not linked to this parent |
| 404 | `{ "error": "Passenger not found" }` | passenger_id doesn't exist |
| 404 | `{ "error": "No active cancellation found for this trip" }` | No `parent_absence_reports` row with matching passenger+session and status `new`/`acknowledged` |
| 422 | `{ "error": "Restore window has passed. Trip starts at HH:MM" }` | Too late — departure time already passed |
| 422 | `{ "error": "Trip already in progress" }` | `route_sessions.started_at` is set |
| 422 | `{ "error": "Passenger has no assigned route" }` | Route not configured |
| 500 | `{ "error": "Failed to restore trip" }` | DB update error |

### Notification visibility after this call
- **Parent** sees the `trip_restored` row via `notifications_select_own` (`recipient_user_id = auth.uid()`)
- **Admins** see it via `rbac_notifications_select`

### Note on absence report status values
`parent_absence_reports.status` constraint: `('new', 'acknowledged', 'resolved', 'cancelled')`
- `new` / `acknowledged` = active cancellation, can be restored
- `cancelled` = voided by parent via this endpoint
- `resolved` = closed by staff — **cannot be restored**
